title: What Questions Should I Ask Before Hiring an App Developer?
excerpt: Hiring the wrong app developer can cost you months and thousands of dollars. These are the critical questions you need to ask any developer or agency before handing over your app project — so you can hire with confidence.
content:
Building a mobile app is a major undertaking. Before you hand your project to any developer or agency, you need to do your homework. The wrong hire can lead to buggy code, missed deadlines, inflated costs, and an app that never actually launches.

Here's what to ask before you commit.

1. What Apps Have You Built Before?

Ask to see live apps in app stores. Download them. Use them. A developer's live work is a far better indicator of quality than any presentation deck.

2. iOS, Android, or Both — What's Your Specialty?

Some developers specialize in one platform. If you need both, ask how they handle it — native builds, React Native, Flutter, or another cross-platform framework.

3. What's Your Development Process?

Look for clear phases: discovery, architecture, design, development, testing, launch, and post-launch support. A structured process protects your timeline and budget.

4. How Do You Handle Bugs and QA Testing?

Every app has bugs. What matters is how they're found and fixed. Ask about their QA process, testing devices, and bug-tracking tools.

5. Who Owns the Code When the Project Is Done?

This is critical. You should own all the code, design assets, and intellectual property at the end of the project. Get this in writing.

6. How Do You Communicate During Development?

Will you get regular demos? Weekly updates? Access to a project management tool? Clear communication prevents nasty surprises.

7. What's Included in Post-Launch Support?

Apps need ongoing maintenance — OS updates, bug fixes, performance improvements. Clarify what's included in the contract and what costs extra.

8. How Do You Handle Scope Changes?

Projects evolve. Ask how changes are priced and approved so you're never blindsided by extra charges.
